name: aligned_transitive_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_aligned_transitive_external_body(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        0 < b,
        0 < c,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(b, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, c),
{
    aligned_transitive(a, b, c);
}
reason: `aligned_transitive` is `external_body` — transitivity of alignment (divisibility) is trusted without proof

name: entry_sizes_aligned_uses_external
reason: The verified `lemma_entry_sizes_aligned` depends on the unverified `aligned_transitive` external_body — if transitivity were wrong the entire alignment chain would be unsound

name: aligned_transitive_chain
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_transitive_chain(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat, d: nat)
    requires
        0 < b,
        0 < c,
        0 < d,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(b, c),
        aligned(c, d),
    ensures
        aligned(a, d),
{
    aligned_transitive(a, b, c);
    aligned_transitive(a, c, d);
}
reason: Two applications of the external_body axiom yield a 3-step transitivity chain — the unverified axiom composes freely to derive alignment across arbitrarily many intermediaries

name: self_aligned_from_lemma
reason: Self-alignment is trivially true but the lemma proves it via nonlinear_arith rather than from `x % x == 0` — tests the base case path

name: adjacent_alignment_from_inv
reason: Adjacent layer divisibility follows from `entry_size(i) == entry_size(i+1) * num_entries(i+1)` — but the proof path goes through the lemma that uses the external_body `aligned_transitive`
